name: Red Rodney
abstract: Robert Roland Chudnick (September 27, 1927 – May 27, 1994), known professionally as Red Rodney, was an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker
Kool Jazz Festival: Musicians for Each Other
JVC Jazz Festival: To Dizzy With Love
JVC Jazz Festival: A Lullaby for Birdland

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: I think he was like a friend even though we never met him. I had never heard him play.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: So I had a chance to meet and play with like Red Rodney and Kenny Barron...
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: I think… I think Red came in for Shorty or came in for Conte or something like that... the guys in the Second Herd didn't want it... I think that's the best white band that ever played music... you’ve got Stan Fishelson, Bernie Glow, Shorty [Rogers], Conte [Candoli] and... Red Rodney.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: There’s still a lot of them there. They never came out. A lot did, but there are a lot that didn’t. So we were the fortunate ones, your Ray Bryants and Stan Getz, Red Rodney, Jimmy Heath, Coltrane, Philly Joe, and those guys. They made it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee refers to 'Red Rodneys' in a general sense ('When the Charlie Venturas and Red Rodneys and whatnot started playing...'), indicating awareness of the type of musician rather than a specific personal relationship.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Red Rodney sat back with the trumpet section and played, and Stan Levey played drums. ... They must have liked the way we sound and they sat in with us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
